tasks.title: Fix infinite loading screen with error handling, timeout, and retry
tasks.description: Harden the init() function in public/js/main.js to handle all failure modes (WebGL unavailable, camera denied, MediaPipe load failure) with clear error messages. Add a 15-second timeout with retry. Add retry button markup and styling.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
- Loading screen disappears and game becomes playable when all prerequisites are met
- Clear error message when camera access is denied
- Clear error message when MediaPipe face landmark model fails to load
- Clear error message when WebGL is unavailable
- 15-second timeout triggers 'failed to load' message with retry option

checklist.description: Add retry button markup to public/index.html
steps: Open public/index.html
Add <button id="retry-btn" class="retry-btn">Retry</button> inside #overlay div after .subtitle
Verify the button is present in the DOM

checklist.description: Add retry button and error overlay CSS to public/css/styles.css
steps: Add #retry-btn styles (hidden by default, styled like existing buttons)
Add #retry-btn.visible { display: inline-block }
Add pointer-events: auto to #overlay.error rule
Verify retry button is hidden by default and visible when .visible class added

checklist.description: Add WebGL pre-check before renderer initialization
steps: Add canvas.getContext('webgl2')/getContext('webgl') check at start of init()
Show clear error message and retry button if WebGL unavailable
Return early from init() on failure

checklist.description: Add specific error handling for camera access denial
steps: Verify existing camera error catch block shows clear message
Add showRetryButton() call to camera error handler
Verify error message explains camera is required

checklist.description: Add specific error handling for MediaPipe face landmark model load failure
steps: Wrap initTracker(stream) call in its own try/catch
Show specific error message about face tracking model failure
Show retry button on MediaPipe failure
Clear timeout on MediaPipe failure

checklist.description: Add 15-second initialization timeout with retry option
steps: Add setTimeout with 15000ms that sets initTimedOut flag and shows error
Show 'Loading is taking too long' message with retry button on timeout
Clear timeout on successful init completion
Check initTimedOut flag after each async step to prevent race conditions
Verify success path does not proceed if timeout already fired

checklist.description: Wire retry button to reload the page
steps: Add click event listener on #retry-btn that calls location.reload()
Verify retry button is accessible (pointer-events enabled on error overlay)

quality_checklist.description: No duplicate error handling logic — showError and showRetryButton are reused consistently
steps: Check that showError() is called with distinct messages for each failure mode
Check that showRetryButton() is a single helper called from all error paths
Verify no inline retry button manipulation outside the helper

quality_checklist.description: Race condition between timeout and success is properly guarded
steps: Verify initTimedOut flag is checked after every await
Verify clearTimeout is called on all non-timeout exit paths (success and error)
Verify success path returns early if initTimedOut is true

quality_checklist.description: Code matches existing style and conventions
steps: Verify indentation matches (2-space)
Verify naming conventions match (camelCase, similar patterns)
Verify no unnecessary changes outside the scope of the fix

checks.description: Loading screen transitions to game when prerequisites are met
steps: Start the app with npm start
Open http://localhost:3000 in browser
Grant camera permission when prompted
Verify the loading overlay disappears and the 3D game is visible and playable

checks.description: Error message shown when camera access is denied
steps: Open http://localhost:3000 in browser
Deny camera permission when prompted
Verify a clear error message about camera access is displayed
Verify a Retry button is visible and clickable
Trigger any error condition (e.g. deny camera)
Click the Retry button
Verify the page reloads (loading screen reappears)

checks.description: WebGL error check exists in code
steps: Read public/js/main.js
Verify there is a WebGL availability check (getContext('webgl')) before initRenderer()
Verify it shows an error message and retry button if WebGL is unavailable
Read public/js/main.js
Verify a setTimeout with 15000ms is set during init()
Verify timeout shows error message and retry button
Verify timeout is cleared on successful completion
